title: alopecurus palustris subsp. geniculatus
intro: Alopecurus palustris subsp. geniculatus, also known as marsh foxtail or marsh fox-tail grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to wetlands and marshy areas in Europe, Asia, and North America. The medicinal properties of Alopecurus palustris subsp. geniculatus include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, and can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction for its herbal benefits.
